name: Lemon-Hazelnut Torte
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200 degrees F.
2. Line two 17 x 11-inch cookie sheets with parchment.
3. Using the springform pan bottom as a guide, trace 2 circles on each parchment sheet and invert them.
4. Finely grind nuts and 1 tablespoon sugar in a processor.
5. Beat whites in a large bowl to soft peaks. Gradually add 1 cup plus 4 tablespoons sugar and beat until stiff and glossy.
6. Gently fold in the nut mixture.
7. Spoon meringue into a large pastry bag fitted with a 1/2-inch plain round tip.
8. Pipe a dab of meringue under the parchment at the corners to secure it to the cookie sheets.
9. Starting at the center of one circle, pipe meringue in a circular motion to form a solid coil and fill the circle. Repeat with remaining three circles.
10. Bake meringues until dry, about 2 1/2 hours. Carefully peel parchment from meringues.
11. Gently place the springform pan bottom atop one meringue to create an 8-inch round disk. Using a serrated knife, cut around the pan, trimming the meringue to an 8-inch round. Repeat with remaining rounds.
12. Whisk lemon juice, yolks, eggs, and 1 cup sugar in a large metal bowl. Set the bowl over simmering water.
13. Whisk constantly until the mixture thickens to the consistency of softly whipped cream and registers 160 degrees F on a candy thermometer, about 13 minutes. Strain the lemon curd through a sieve.
14. Press plastic wrap onto the surface; chill until cold.
15. Beat cream in a medium bowl to soft peaks. Add 1 tablespoon sugar and beat until stiff.
16. Fold the whipped cream into the chilled lemon curd.
17. Construction: Place one meringue in the bottom of the springform pan, trimming the edges with a knife to fit into the pan. Spoon 1 1/4 cups lemon cream over it. Place the second meringue in the pan, trimming if needed, and gently press down until the meringue touches the cream. Spoon 1 1/4 cups lemon cream over. Top with the third meringue and repeat the process.
18. Freeze the torte overnight. (Can be made 2 days ahead. Wrap tightly and keep frozen.)
19. Raspberry Sauce: Puree raspberries in a processor. Strain into a bowl, pressing on seeds with the back of a spoon. Mix in sugar and lemon juice. Refrigerate until cold, about 1 hour.
20. Serving: Wrap a hot wet towel around the pan sides. Release. Transfer to a platter; run an icing spatula around the sides to smooth out. Arrange raspberries atop the tart. Serve with sauce.
